name: Ehyeh
notes: Standalone self-designation in addition to the full formula.

name: YHWH Echad
notes: Shema identity formula rather than a simple lexical name.

name: El Elohei Yisrael
notes: Exact altar-name declaration with debated syntax.

name: Qadosh
notes: The verse uses Qadosh as the divine speaker designation.

name: Eben Yisrael
notes: Exact words but their syntactic relation and epithet status are debated.

name: YHWH Tzevaot Hu Melekh HaKavod
notes: Full identity formula rather than a compact name.

name: Ani Rishon vaAni Acharon
notes: I am first and I am last.

name: Elohei HaRuchot LeKhol Basar
notes: God of the spirits of all flesh with source-defective spelling.

name: YHWH Tzevaot Shemo
notes: YHWH of Hosts is his name.

name: YHWH Tsidkenu
notes: Applied in a royal or messianic naming context and requires object review.

name: YHWH Shammah
notes: Name of the envisioned city rather than a direct divine address.

name: Malakh YHWH
notes: Christian identification as divine requires an explicit theological rule.

name: Pele Yoetz
notes: Part of the Isaianic throne-name sequence.

name: Avi Ad
notes: Part of the Isaianic throne-name sequence.

name: Isaiah Throne-Name Sequence
hebrew: פלא יועץ אל גבור אבי עד שר שלום
notes: Full sequence tested as one formula.

name: Yeshua HaMashiach
notes: Conventional Hebrew Christian title but the New Testament textual source is Greek.

name: Adoneinu Yeshua HaMashiach defective
notes: Defective Adoneinu spelling in the cited Hebrew translation.

name: Adoneinu Yeshua HaMashiach plene
notes: Plene control for the translation-sensitive title.

name: Ben HaElohim defective
notes: Defective Elohim spelling used in older Hebrew translations.

name: Ben HaElohim plene
notes: Modern plene control for Son of God.

name: Davar HaElohim defective
notes: Defective Elohim spelling in the Word of God title.

name: Davar HaElohim plene
notes: Modern plene control for Word of God.

name: Amen lexical form
notes: Bare Semitic title extracted from the exact translated form HaAmen and therefore not source-exact as displayed.

name: HaRoeh HaTov defective
notes: Defective Roeh spelling in one Hebrew translation.

name: Ben HaAdam
notes: Son of Man; the source prints the phrase with a maqaf.

name: Melekh HaOlam
notes: King of the universe with article.

name: HaShem
notes: The Name; a common non-liturgical Jewish circumlocution.

name: Shalom
notes: Talmud identifies Shalom as a divine name.

name: Qudsha Berikh Hu
notes: Aramaic counterpart to HaKadosh Baruch Hu.

name: HaAmen
notes: Exact article-bearing Hebrew translation control for the bare Amen title.
